title: Neanotis gracilis
taxonomy: Neanotis gracilis is classified within the family Rubiaceae. This family is a large and diverse group of flowering plants commonly known as the coffee or madder family. Neanotis gracilis belongs to the genus Neanotis, a group of plants native to the tropical regions of Asia. The genus Neanotis is further classified within the tribe Neanotideae. Neanotis gracilis is a species of flowering plant that is closely related to other species within the same genus and family.

intro: Neanotis gracilis, also known as Indian snakeroot, is a plant species belonging to the family Rubiaceae, characterized by its slender stems and small, white or pale yellow flowers. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may contribute to its use in treating various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. Bioactive constituents of Neanotis gracilis include flavonoids, glycosides, and terpenoids, which have been identified as key contributors to its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, the plant is often prepared as a decoction, infusion, or dried powder, and is also used in the form of ointments and poultices to treat skin conditions and wounds.
